methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Andrenam is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Matej Cernosek and Alex Chu that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI—predominantly at the Seed and Series A stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. The peer-median method examines five same-sector comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, NODA AI at $125M, and Tenna Systems at $60M), producing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $60M to $500M. The disparity reflects the wide spread in Defense & Security valuations across development stages.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $21M to $172M, with a median point estimate of $88M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around market timing, customer traction, and relative positioning within a heterogeneous peer set. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to near-term capital history, while the peer-median incorporates observed market pricing but relies on limited seed-stage public data. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and differentiation relative to the named peer set.
